seo.title: Virginia Dietitian/Nutritionist Requirements — Title Protection (2026)
seo.description: Virginia does not require a state-specific dietitian license. Title protection is managed through DHP qualifications. CDR registration is the primary credential.
seo.keywords: Virginia dietitian license, VA dietitian requirements, Virginia Department of Health Professions DHP, Virginia dietitian title protection

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: No state application fee. CDR exam fee is $200 nationally.
quick_facts.renewal_cycle: No state renewal. CDR registration renews every 5 years with 75 CPE hours.

license_types.title: Title Protection — Dietitian / Nutritionist
license_types.description: Virginia does not mandate state licensure for dietitians. However, the DHP Board of Health Professions establishes minimum qualifications for using the protected titles 'dietitian' or 'nutritionist'. Meeting CDR registration standards or equivalent qualifications allows use of the title.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete 1,200 hours of supervised practice through a CDR-approved dietetic internship.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the CDR Registration Examination for Dietitians and obtain active RD/RDN status.

steps.title: Use the Protected Title
steps.description: With active CDR registration, you are qualified to use the legally protected titles 'dietitian' or 'nutritionist' in Virginia. No separate state application is needed.

documents.detail: Your active CDR card serves as the primary credential in Virginia.
Proof of your accredited degree program and supervised practice completion.

provisional_text: Because Virginia does not issue a state license, there is no provisional or temporary state credential.
reciprocity_text: Virginia has no state license to transfer. Title protection eligibility is demonstrated by holding a valid CDR credential, or a current dietitian/nutritionist license or certificate from another state.

faqs.question: Does Virginia require a state dietitian license?
faqs.answer: No. Virginia operates under a title-protection model. To use the title 'dietitian' or 'nutritionist', you must meet the qualifications set by the DHP Board of Health Professions, most commonly by holding active CDR registration.

faqs.question: What does it cost to practice as a dietitian in Virginia?
faqs.answer: There is no state fee. The CDR national exam costs $200, and the CDR annual maintenance fee is $80 (increasing to $90 in 2027).

faqs.question: Is Virginia moving towards formal state licensure?
faqs.answer: House Bill 576 was introduced in 2026 to enable Virginia to join the Dietitian Licensure Compact, but the bill was left in committee as of February 2026. Virginia does not currently have an active licensure pathway.
